prompt: What is the maximum allowable leak rate for comfort cooling appliances containing 50 or more pounds of refrigerant?
explanation: EPA leak repair rules set a 10% annual leak rate threshold for comfort cooling appliances with 50 or more pounds of refrigerant.

distractorExplanations.b: 15% applies to other equipment categories, not comfort cooling at this threshold.
distractorExplanations.c: 20% is the commercial refrigeration threshold for large systems, not comfort cooling.
distractorExplanations.d: 30% exceeds any EPA annual leak rate allowance for regulated appliances.

prompt: A technician will cut and braze a leaking liquid line on a packaged rooftop unit containing 12 pounds of R-410A. What recovery level is required before opening the system?

options.text: Recover 80% of charge by weight only

explanation: For high-pressure appliances with more than 5 pounds of refrigerant opened for major repair, EPA requires recovery to 15 inches of mercury vacuum (or equivalent evacuation level for the appliance type).

distractorExplanations.a: 0 psig leaves refrigerant in the system and does not meet evacuation requirements before opening.
distractorExplanations.b: Four inches of mercury is below the required evacuation level for this high-pressure major repair scenario.
distractorExplanations.d: EPA specifies evacuation levels, not a percentage-only recovery shortcut before opening the circuit.
